assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Includes command `kubectl get events --all-namespaces | grep -i deprecat` to check for deprecated API usage

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: No mention of admission webhooks or validating/mutating webhook configurations that could cause latency or rejections

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Includes resource constraint checks with commands like `kubectl describe nodes | grep -A 5 "Conditions:\|Allocated resources:"` and `kubectl top nodes/pods`

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Multiple commands check kube-system pods status including `kubectl get pods -n kube-system` and looking for crashlooping components

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: While it checks API server logs and pod metrics with kubectl top, it doesn't include the specific `kubectl get --raw /metrics` command for API server metrics

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Includes checking network policies with `kubectl get networkpolicies --all-namespaces` and service mesh components like istio-system

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check events (`kubectl get events`) and specifically mentions checking readiness/liveness probes with `kubectl describe pods`

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: No mention of HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) components or their potential behavior changes
